# Test-Quality Review: daily_os_next/agents/state

**Scope:** `lib/features/daily_os_next/agents/state/` (1 impl file + 1 generated file) and
`test/features/daily_os_next/agents/state/` (1 test file).

Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis).

---

## File inventory

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| `lib/.../state/day_agent_providers.dart` | 127 | Yes | `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` has no dedicated wiring test |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[LOW]** `day_agent_providers.dart` (127 lines) and `day_agent_providers_test.dart` (353 lines) are both well within acceptable bounds. No file split is needed.
  **RESOLVED:** confirmed true — both files are small; no split warranted.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart` — each of the 8 test bodies independently constructs `MockXxx` dependencies, `ProviderContainer`, and `addTearDown`. This is ~35 repeated local variable declarations across ~330 lines. Extract a file-level `_buildContainer({required ...overrides})` helper that accepts only the varying overrides as named parameters. The pattern is identical to what `AGENTS.md` calls out explicitly ("Extract pump/setup helpers"). This is the single biggest quality gap in the state test file. **RESOLVED (adapted):** extracted a shared `buildContainer(overrides)` helper (ProviderContainer + auto-dispose) used by all 9 wiring tests; the per-test mock declarations stay local because each provider family wires a different dependency set — folding them into one parameter object would obscure which dependencies each provider actually consumes.
- [x] **[MED]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart:21-61` — `dayAgentServiceProvider` wiring test asserts all six dependency identities via `same(...)` and verifies the notification callback. The same mock + container + callback pattern is copy-pasted verbatim in the `dayAgentPlanServiceProvider` test at lines 63–97. These two tests are similar enough that a shared `_buildServiceContainer` helper with a `dependencies` map would eliminate the duplication.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** the two providers wire genuinely different dependency sets (`agentService`/`orchestrator`/`templateService` vs `journalDb`) and assert different fields, so a unified `dependencies`-map container would obscure which deps each provider actually consumes. The part that *was* copy-pasted verbatim across all three service-wiring tests — firing `onPersistedStateChanged` and verifying `notifyUiOnly({id, agentNotification})` — is now factored into a shared `expectPersistedStateNotification(...)` helper used by the service, plan, and capture tests.
- [x] **[MED]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart` — `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` (lines 32–45 of the impl) is never tested in its own `group`. It is only used as an override in other provider tests. The wiring test should verify that `DayAgentCaptureService` receives `agentRepository`, `syncService`, `journalDb`, `journalRepository`, `fts5Db`, `orchestrator`, `domainLogger`, and the notifications callback — consistent with how `dayAgentServiceProvider` and `dayAgentPlanServiceProvider` are verified. This is a genuine missing-behavior gap.
  **RESOLVED:** already addressed by the `[HIGH]` resolution below — a dedicated `group('dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider', ...)` exists, overriding all seven Riverpod deps (with `Fts5Db` registered through `getIt`), asserting each wired field via `same(...)` and now verifying the persisted-state notification through the shared `expectPersistedStateNotification(...)` helper. This MED item is a duplicate of the resolved HIGH gap.
- [x] **[LOW]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart:99-127` — `dayAgentProvider` test uses `makeTestIdentity` and asserts `result == identity`, then verifies `getDayAgentForDate` was called exactly once. The test is meaningful and DRY given its single scenario, but a "returns null when service returns null" negative path is absent.
  **RESOLVED:** added `'propagates null when no day agent exists for the date'` — stubs `getDayAgentForDate` to return `null`, asserts the provider future resolves to `null`, and verifies the service was queried once.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[LOW]** The providers in this file are all Riverpod wiring glue (dependency injection + async delegation). There is no pure domain logic, state machine, or data transformation to exercise generatively. Glados is not a fit here.
  **RESOLVED:** confirmed true — providers are pure DI/delegation glue with no generatable pure logic; no Glados test added.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` — the provider is defined at `lib/.../state/day_agent_providers.dart:32-45` and wires `agentRepository`, `syncService`, `journalDb`, `journalRepository`, `fts5Db`, `orchestrator`, `domainLogger`, and notifications. None of these wiring assignments are verified by a dedicated test group. Only its service object is used as an override in other tests. **RESOLVED:** new 'dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider wires dependencies and persisted-state notifications' test — overrides all seven Riverpod dependencies (Fts5Db swapped in via `setUpTestGetIt`), asserts each wired field is `same(...)` as its override, and verifies `onPersistedStateChanged` forwards through `notifyUiOnly` with the agent notification.
- [x] **[MED]** `dayAgentProvider` negative path — `getDayAgentForDate` returning `null` is not tested. The provider propagates `null` directly; a test would catch any inadvertent null-guard change.
  **RESOLVED:** covered by the new `'propagates null when no day agent exists for the date'` test in the `dayAgentProvider` group (same fix as the LOW duplicate above) — asserts the future resolves to `null` and the service is called once.
- [x] **[LOW]** `parsedItemsForCaptureProvider` — the "capture not found" branch (`getCapture` returns `null`, so `agentUpdateStreamProvider` is never watched) is not exercised. The provider silently falls through to `parsedItemsForCapture` even when no capture exists; this path should be explicitly tested.
  **RESOLVED:** added `'still loads parsed items when the capture is not found'` — stubs `getCapture` to return `null`, asserts the provider still calls `parsedItemsForCapture` (returning empty here) and resolves without watching the agent-scoped update stream.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[LOW]** All tests are synchronous Riverpod container reads (`.future` awaited directly); no real I/O, no timers, no `Future.delayed`. No fake-time violations detected.
  **RESOLVED:** confirmed true — no timers/delays/real I/O; the two added tests follow the same await-the-future pattern. No fake-time work needed.
- [x] **[LOW]** The repeated mock object creation in each test body (8 times × 3–8 `MockXxx()` instantiations) adds minor per-test overhead, but the mocks themselves are trivial to construct. Extracting the shared helper (see Test Quality section) would incidentally eliminate this overhead.
  **RESOLVED:** the shared `buildContainer(...)` helper already removed the per-test container/teardown boilerplate. The `MockXxx()` instantiations stay local on purpose — each provider consumes a different dependency set, so keeping the relevant mocks visible in each test body documents exactly what that provider wires; the construction cost is negligible.

---

## Summary

- 0 oversized impl files; 0 oversized test files.
- 1 genuine missing-behavior gap: `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` has no wiring test (`[HIGH]`).
- 1 major DRY violation: 8 tests each build their own `ProviderContainer` inline; a shared helper is required (`[HIGH]`).
- 2 negative-path gaps: `dayAgentProvider`/`parsedItemsForCaptureProvider` null-return branches untested (`[MED]`/`[LOW]`).
- No Glados opportunities (pure wiring code), no fake-time violations, all mocks come from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.
- Biggest opportunity: extract a `_buildContainer` factory to collapse ~300 lines of boilerplate into reusable setup.
